title: Brookstone Queen Headboard
summary: A plank-panel headboard with mitered X-style cross supports on the back — the queen-size scale-up of the Brookstone twin headboard.
description:
The headboard face is eighteen 1x4 boards edge to edge, alternating bark-side up and down as you assemble them — a real trick for keeping a wide glued-up-looking panel flatter over time, since wood from opposite growth-ring orientations cups in opposite directions and tends to cancel out rather than compound. Nail or screw the top and bottom trim on from the back if you don't have a nailer, which keeps every fastener on the show face hidden.

The back cross supports are the one part worth double-checking against the actual build rather than a summary number: cut them long (36-3/4"), miter both ends at 45° parallel to each other, then trim a small notch 2" in from the top end square to the top edge — that notch is what lets the support tuck under the top trim rather than butting into it.

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 45° off square, parallel. Then measure in 2" from the top end and cut a line square to the top edge, per the last step.

steps.title: Assemble the headboard panel
steps.body: Line up all eighteen panel boards, alternating the grain (bark side up, then down, board to board) to help the panel stay flat over time. Adjust for square, then glue and nail the top and bottom trim in place. If you don't have a nailer, screw from the back so no screw holes show on the front.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Screw the side and front legs on with glue and 2" screws.

steps.title: Attach the leg fronts
steps.body: A nailer is handy here; otherwise screw the leg front pieces to the legs with glue and 2" screws or nails.

steps.title: Attach the front trim
steps.body: Measure and cut the front trim to fit rather than trusting a fixed number. Nails hide best — if staining and you don't have a nailer, use a hammer and finish nails to keep holes minimal; if painting, screws and glue are fine.

steps.title: Attach the top trim and header
steps.body: Add the top trim with screws or nails and glue, centered on the top. Then attach the header to the top trim with finish nails and glue, also centered.

steps.title: Cut and attach the cross supports
steps.body: Cut two 1x4s to 36-3/4" long with both ends mitered at 45° off square, parallel to each other. On the top end of each, measure in 2" and mark a line square to the top edge; cut this line to form the notch that tucks under the top trim. Attach the cross supports to the back of the headboard with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, nailing into every panel board they cross.

images.alt: Queen-size plank-panel headboard with angled cross supports on the back
